title: Ly Thuong Kiet Fighting the Song Army
summary: The famous general Ly Thuong Kiet led the Ly Dynasty's army and people to crush the Song invasion, featuring the legendary poem 'Nam Quoc Son Ha'.

slides.text: Ly Thuong Kiet was a brilliant general who took the initiative by advancing into Song territory to block their invasion plans.
slides.imagePrompt: General Ly Thuong Kiet riding a horse, pointing his sword forward, leading an army through a pass, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: At the Nhu Nguyet River defense line, the enemy was forced to a halt by the staunch resistance of our troops.
slides.imagePrompt: The defensive line at Nhu Nguyet river, soldiers guarding with spears and shields, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: In the dead of night, a divine poem echoed from a nearby temple: 'The Southern Mountains and Rivers are where the Southern Emperor dwells...', proclaiming the nation's sovereignty.
slides.imagePrompt: An ancient temple by the river at night, mystical voice manifesting the poem 'Nam Quoc Son Ha', glowing text in the sky, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The Song soldiers were confused and petrified upon hearing the poem, and were subsequently defeated by our counter-attack, forcing them to retreat to their country.
slides.imagePrompt: Terrified Song soldiers retreating across the river, victory for Dai Viet army, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: At which river did the poem 'Nam Quoc Son Ha' echo?
What did Ly Thuong Kiet do to block the invaders?
options: Take the initiative to advance into Song territory
Wait for the enemy to arrive before fighting
slides.answer: Take the initiative to advance into Song territory
